flags.concern: Create-task resolver persistence: Step 2 only names `ai-timeline-agent` plumbing plus `shared/lineage.ts`, but the real task rows for transfer-style edits are serialized in the `create-task` resolvers. `imageGenerationResolver.buildTaskParams()` rebuilds params from an explicit whitelist, and `setTaskLineageFields()` does not carry any placement fields today, so a new `timeline_placement` object would still be dropped before persistence unless those resolver files are added to scope.
flags.evidence: In [imageGeneration.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/imageGeneration.ts#L190), `buildTaskParams()` returns a fresh object with prompt/resolution/shot fields only. In [lineage.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/shared/lineage.ts#L3), `TaskLineageFields` only covers shot/basedOn/sourceVariant/createAsGeneration/toolType.

flags.concern: Verification scope: the planned tests do not exercise the persistence boundary where placement intent is most likely to disappear. The listed `ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.test.ts` suite mocks `createGenerationTask()`, and `complete_task/handler.test.ts` mocks `createGenerationFromTask()`, so those tests can all pass without ever asserting that the real `create-task` resolvers wrote `timeline_placement` into stored task params.
flags.evidence: In [create-task.test.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.test.ts#L3), `createGenerationTask` is fully mocked. In [handler.test.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/complete_task/handler.test.ts#L13), `createGenerationFromTask` is mocked as well. There are no resolver-level tests under `supabase/functions/create-task/` for the families this plan relies on.
